firmware.name: Smoothieware
firmware.description: Open-source firmware for CNC and 3D printing, running on ARM Cortex-M processors (LPC1768/LPC1769). Uses a modular architecture with configurable modules. Config-file based settings rather than $ commands.

codes.name: Rapid Move
codes.description: Rapid move to the specified position. In Smoothieware, G0 uses a separately configurable maximum rate (alpha_max_rate, beta_max_rate, gamma_max_rate in config). In laser mode, G0 moves with the laser OFF regardless of the current S value.

parameters.name: F
parameters.description: Feed rate in mm/min (optional for G0, overrides rapid rate)

crossReferences.note: Similar behavior. Smoothieware adds E axis for extruder control. Both disable laser during G0 in laser mode.
In RepRapFirmware, G0 and G1 are identical. In Smoothieware, G0 uses separate max rates and disables laser.

modeNotes.description: G0 moves automatically disable the laser (PWM output is 0), preventing burning during positioning moves. This matches grbl's laser mode behavior.

codes.notes: Smoothieware treats G0 as a rapid move with configurable per-axis maximum rates defined in the config file. G0 is modal.

codes.name: Linear Move
codes.description: Controlled straight-line move at the specified feed rate. In laser mode, G1 moves with the laser ON at the current S power level.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Laser power (in laser mode, can be set inline)

examples.description: Linear move with laser power at 0.8

crossReferences.note: Same behavior for XYZ. Smoothieware adds E axis. Both support inline S for laser power.
RepRapFirmware also supports inline S for laser. Both add E axis for extrusion.

modeNotes.description: Laser is active during G1 moves at the power level set by M3 S value or inline S parameter. Unlike grbl, there is no M4 dynamic power mode — power does NOT automatically scale with speed.

codes.notes: Feed rate is modal. In laser mode, G1 fires the laser while G0 does not — this G0/G1 distinction is the primary way to control laser on/off during motion.

codes.name: Clockwise Arc
codes.description: Clockwise circular interpolation in the currently selected plane (G17/G18/G19). Arc center is specified by IJK offsets or R radius.

parameters.name: I
parameters.description: Arc center offset in X (incremental from current position)

parameters.name: J
parameters.description: Arc center offset in Y (incremental from current position)

parameters.name: K
parameters.description: Arc center offset in Z (incremental from current position)

examples.description: CW arc to X10 Y10 with center offset I5 J0
CW arc to X10 Y10 with radius 5

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. Both use incremental IJK offsets (G91.1 mode only).
Same behavior. LinuxCNC additionally supports absolute IJK mode (G90.1).

modeNotes.description: Laser is active during arc moves at the current S power level.

codes.notes: Only incremental IJK mode is supported. Use either IJK offsets or R radius, not both. Arc segments are internally rendered as small line segments.

codes.name: Counter-Clockwise Arc
codes.description: Counter-clockwise circular interpolation. Same as G2 but in the opposite direction.

examples.description: CCW arc to X10 Y10 with radius 5

codes.name: Dwell
codes.description: Pauses execution for a specified duration. P interpretation depends on config: in RepRap mode (default), P is milliseconds. In GRBL-compatible mode, P is seconds. S always means seconds.

parameters.name: P
parameters.description: Dwell time — milliseconds in RepRap mode (default), seconds in GRBL mode

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Dwell time in seconds (always seconds regardless of mode)

examples.description: Pause for 1 second (GRBL mode, P=seconds) OR 1 millisecond (RepRap mode) — AMBIGUOUS

crossReferences.note: grbl uses P in seconds. Smoothieware P interpretation depends on config mode. Use S parameter for unambiguous seconds across both.
LinuxCNC uses P in seconds. Smoothieware RepRap mode uses P in milliseconds.
Both use P in milliseconds and S in seconds in RepRap mode.

codes.notes: WARNING: P parameter interpretation varies by firmware mode. Use S (seconds) for cross-firmware compatibility. In RepRap mode (grbl_mode false), P=milliseconds. In GRBL mode (grbl_mode true), P=seconds.

codes.name: Firmware Retract
codes.description: Performs a firmware-controlled retraction of the extruder filament. Retraction parameters are configured with M207. Part of the firmware retraction feature used in 3D printing.

examples.description: Retract filament using firmware retraction settings

crossReferences.note: RepRapFirmware also supports G10 for firmware retraction, but G10 is overloaded — with P/L parameters it sets tool offsets or coordinate systems. Smoothieware separates these: G10 alone = retract, G10 L2/L20 = coordinate systems.

codes.notes: Requires the extruder module to be loaded. Retraction length and speed are configured via M207. Using firmware retraction allows the slicer to output G10/G11 commands instead of explicit E moves, making it easy to tune retraction settings without re-slicing.

codes.name: Firmware Unretract
codes.description: Performs a firmware-controlled un-retraction (prime) of the extruder filament. Unretraction parameters are configured with M208.
codes.notes: Unretraction length can include extra prime length configured via M208.

codes.name: Set Work Coordinate Offset (Absolute)
codes.description: Sets a work coordinate system offset (G54-G59.3) to absolute values.

examples.code: G10 L2 P1 X10 Y20 Z0
examples.description: Set G54 offset to X10 Y20 Z0

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. grbl supports P1-P6 (G54-G59). Smoothieware supports P1-P9 (G54-G59.3).

codes.notes: Smoothieware supports 9 coordinate systems (G54-G59.3), more than grbl's 6.

codes.name: Set Work Coordinate Offset (Relative to Current Position)
codes.description: Sets a work coordinate system offset such that the current position becomes the specified coordinate value.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: Desired X position at current location

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Desired Y position at current location

parameters.name: Z
parameters.description: Desired Z position at current location

examples.code: G10 L20 P1 X0 Y0 Z0
examples.description: Set G54 so current position reads as origin

codes.name: XY Plane Selection
codes.description: Selects the XY plane for arc commands (G2/G3). This is the default plane.
codes.notes: Default plane. Arcs use I and J offsets in this plane.

codes.name: XZ Plane Selection
codes.description: Selects the XZ plane for arc commands (G2/G3).
codes.notes: Arcs use I and K offsets in this plane.

codes.name: YZ Plane Selection
codes.description: Selects the YZ plane for arc commands (G2/G3).
codes.notes: Arcs use J and K offsets in this plane.

codes.name: Inch Mode
codes.description: Sets all subsequent coordinates and feed rates to inches.
codes.notes: Modal. Affects coordinate values and feed rates.

codes.name: Millimeter Mode
codes.description: Sets all subsequent coordinates and feed rates to millimeters. This is the default.
codes.notes: Default unit mode for Smoothieware.

codes.name: Home Axes
codes.description: Homes the specified axes (or all axes if none specified) by moving toward endstops. WARNING: This is NOT the same as G28 in grbl/LinuxCNC, which moves to a stored position.

examples.description: Home only X and Y axes

crossReferences.note: CRITICAL CONFLICT: Smoothieware G28 = homing (equivalent to grbl's $H command). grbl's G28 moves to a stored position — completely different behavior. Sending grbl G-code with G28 to a Smoothieware machine will trigger an unexpected homing cycle.
CRITICAL CONFLICT: LinuxCNC G28 moves to a stored predefined position. Smoothieware G28 homes to endstops.
Same homing behavior as Smoothieware. Both follow the RepRap/3D-printing convention.

codes.notes: CRITICAL CROSS-FIRMWARE CONFLICT: G28 in Smoothieware performs homing (moves to endstops). In grbl and LinuxCNC, G28 moves to a stored predefined position. This is the single biggest source of cross-firmware confusion. Homing direction and speed are configured in the config file (alpha_homing_direction, alpha_fast_homing_rate, etc.).

codes.name: Park Position Move
codes.description: Moves to a predefined park position. This is closer to the NIST G28 stored-position behavior.

crossReferences.note: Smoothieware G28.1 is functionally similar to grbl's G28 (move to stored position).

codes.notes: Park position is configured in the config file. Use this instead of G28 when you want to move to a predefined position without homing.

codes.name: Home Axes (Explicit)
codes.description: Homes the specified axes. Functionally identical to G28 but explicitly signals homing intent.
codes.notes: Alternative homing command. Axes must be specified explicitly.

codes.name: Manual Homing / Set Position
codes.description: Sets the specified axis positions without moving. Used for manual homing when no endstops are available.

examples.description: Tell firmware current position is origin (manual home)

codes.notes: Useful for machines without endstops. Sets the current position to the given coordinates and marks the axis as homed.

codes.name: Home Axes (Fast Only)
codes.description: Homes using only the fast homing rate, skipping the slow approach phase.
codes.notes: Less accurate than normal homing (G28) since it skips the slow approach. Useful for rough homing when speed matters more than precision.

codes.name: Home Axes (With Homing Order)
codes.description: Homes axes in the order specified by the homing_order config setting.

examples.description: Home axes using configured homing order

codes.notes: Uses homing_order from config to determine axis homing sequence.

codes.name: Home Individual Axis
codes.description: Homes a single specified axis.

codes.name: Home with Stallguard
codes.description: Homes using stepper driver stallguard (sensorless homing). Requires compatible stepper drivers (e.g., TMC2130, TMC2209).

examples.description: Home X and Y using stallguard

codes.notes: Sensorless homing — requires TMC stepper drivers with stallguard capability.

codes.name: Bed Leveling / Auto-Level
codes.description: Runs an automatic bed leveling probe routine. Probes a grid of points and applies compensation. Requires the ZProbe module.

crossReferences.note: Similar auto-leveling purpose. Implementation details differ.

codes.notes: Grid size and probe parameters are configured in the config file (leveling-strategy settings). Stores compensation data that is applied to all subsequent moves.

codes.name: Single Z Probe
codes.description: Probes downward at the current XY position to determine the Z height. Reports the probed position. WARNING: Different from NIST/grbl G30.

parameters.name: Z
parameters.description: Target probe depth (optional, defaults to config)

crossReferences.note: CONFLICT: grbl G30 moves to a second stored position (like G28 but with a different stored location). Smoothieware G30 performs a Z probe. Completely different behavior.
CONFLICT: LinuxCNC G30 moves to a second predefined position. Smoothieware G30 is a Z probe.

codes.notes: CROSS-FIRMWARE CONFLICT: Smoothieware G30 = Z probe. grbl/LinuxCNC G30 = move to second stored position. Requires ZProbe module. Probe speed and other parameters are configured in the config file.

codes.name: Delta Calibration
codes.description: Runs delta auto-calibration routine. Probes multiple points and adjusts delta geometry parameters (endstop offsets, arm length, delta radius, etc.).
codes.notes: Delta kinematics only. Iteratively probes and adjusts delta parameters. Use M665/M666 to view and manually set delta parameters. Results can be saved with M500.

codes.name: Probe Toward Workpiece (Stop on Contact, Error if No Contact)
codes.description: Moves toward the target position and stops when the probe input triggers. Errors if probe does not trigger before reaching the target.

examples.description: Probe down to Z-10 at 100mm/min, error if no contact

codes.notes: Standard NIST probing command. Requires ZProbe module.

codes.name: Probe Toward Workpiece (Stop on Contact, No Error)
codes.description: Same as G38.2 but does not error if the probe does not trigger.

examples.description: Probe down to Z-10, no error if no contact

codes.name: Probe Away from Workpiece (Stop on Loss of Contact, Error)
codes.description: Moves away from workpiece, stops when probe deactivates. Errors if probe does not deactivate.

examples.description: Probe upward until probe releases, error if no release

codes.name: Probe Away from Workpiece (Stop on Loss of Contact, No Error)
codes.description: Same as G38.4 but does not error if the probe does not deactivate.

examples.description: Probe upward until probe releases, no error

codes.name: Baby Steps / Z Offset
codes.description: Applies a Z offset (baby stepping) to all subsequent moves. Used for fine-tuning first layer height during 3D printing.

examples.description: Apply -0.1mm Z offset (move nozzle closer to bed)

crossReferences.note: LinuxCNC G43 is tool length offset from tool table. Smoothieware G43.2 is a simpler baby-step Z offset, not a full tool length compensation system.

codes.notes: Commonly used to adjust first layer height while printing. Offset persists until cleared with G43.2 Z0 or reset.

codes.name: Move in Machine Coordinates
codes.description: Next move uses machine (absolute) coordinates, ignoring any work coordinate system offsets (G54-G59.3) and G92 offsets. Non-modal — only affects the line it appears on.

examples.description: Rapid Z to machine zero (useful for tool changes)

codes.notes: Non-modal — must be repeated on each line where machine coordinates are desired. Must be used with G0 or G1 on the same line.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 1
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 1. This is the default coordinate system.
codes.notes: Default WCS. Set offsets with G10 L2 P1 or G10 L20 P1.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 2
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 2.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 3
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 3.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 4
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 4.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 5
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 5.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 6
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 6.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 7
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 7.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. grbl does not support G59.1-G59.3.

codes.notes: Extended WCS not available in grbl (grbl only supports G54-G59).

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 8
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 8.

codes.name: Work Coordinate System 9
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 9.

codes.name: Absolute Distance Mode
codes.description: Set absolute positioning mode for XYZ axes. All coordinate values are interpreted as absolute positions.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior for XYZ. Smoothieware additionally supports M82/M83 for independent extruder positioning mode.

codes.notes: In Smoothieware, G90/G91 control XYZ positioning mode. Extruder (E) positioning mode is controlled independently by M82 (absolute) and M83 (relative). This is different from grbl where G90/G91 affects all axes.

codes.name: Incremental Distance Mode
codes.description: Set incremental (relative) positioning mode for XYZ axes. All coordinate values are interpreted as offsets from the current position.
codes.notes: Does not affect extruder positioning mode — use M82/M83 for that. This independence is important for 3D printing where XYZ may be absolute while E is relative.

codes.name: Set Position (Coordinate Offset)
codes.description: Sets the current position to the given values by applying a coordinate offset. Does not move any axes. The offset is applied on top of the active work coordinate system.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. Both apply an offset, not a true machine position change.
Same behavior per NIST standard. LinuxCNC G92 offsets persist across power cycles; Smoothieware behavior depends on config.

codes.notes: G92 E0 is extremely common in 3D printing G-code to reset the extruder position. For CNC work, prefer G10 L20 to set WCS offsets instead of G92.

codes.name: Clear G92 Offset
codes.description: Clears (zeroes) all G92 coordinate offsets.

codes.name: Clear G92 Offset (Alternate)
codes.description: Clears G92 coordinate offsets. Functionally equivalent to G92.1 in Smoothieware.

codes.name: Feed Rate Mode: Units Per Minute
codes.description: Sets the feed rate interpretation to units per minute (mm/min or inches/min depending on G20/G21). This is the default and only supported feed rate mode.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. grbl also supports G93 (inverse time); Smoothieware does not.
Same behavior. LinuxCNC also supports G93 and G95.

codes.notes: Default feed rate mode. Smoothieware does not support G93 (inverse time) or G95 (units per revolution) feed rate modes.

codes.name: Program Stop
codes.description: Pauses the program and waits for user input (button press or resume command) to continue. All motion stops and heaters remain on.

examples.description: Pause and wait for user to resume

crossReferences.note: Same behavior — pauses program. grbl requires cycle start (~) to resume.

codes.notes: Useful for tool changes or manual interventions mid-program.

codes.name: Optional Program Stop
codes.description: Optional stop — only pauses if the optional stop switch/setting is enabled.

codes.name: Spindle On (CW) / Laser On
codes.description: Turns on the spindle clockwise or enables the laser. Behavior depends on whether the spindle module or laser module is configured. S parameter sets speed/power. Laser power range depends on maximum_s_value config setting (commonly 1.0, 100, or 255).

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Spindle speed (RPM) or laser power. Range is 0 to maximum_s_value (configured in config file, default 1.0)

examples.description: Turn on laser at 80% power (if maximum_s_value is 1.0)
Turn on laser at ~78% power (if maximum_s_value is 255)

crossReferences.note: In grbl, M3 is constant power mode (laser stays at set power). grbl also has M4 for dynamic power that scales with speed. Smoothieware has NO M4 dynamic power mode — M3 and M4 are identical for laser.

modeNotes.description: Laser power is set by S parameter. The range depends on maximum_s_value in config (1.0 means S0.0 to S1.0, 255 means S0 to S255). Unlike grbl, there is no dynamic power mode — power does not automatically scale with acceleration/deceleration.
S sets spindle RPM. Direction is clockwise.

codes.notes: IMPORTANT: Smoothieware does NOT have grbl's M3/M4 distinction for laser. grbl M4 (dynamic power) scales laser output with speed during acceleration — this prevents over-burn at corners. Smoothieware has no equivalent; laser power is always proportional to the S value regardless of speed. Configure maximum_s_value in config to match your G-code sender's expected S range.

codes.name: Spindle Off / Laser Off
codes.description: Turns off the spindle or laser.

codes.name: Enable Stepper Motors
codes.description: Enables (energizes) all stepper motors. Motors will hold position and resist movement.
codes.notes: Motors are automatically enabled when motion commands are received. M17 is useful for explicitly re-enabling motors after M18/M84.

codes.name: Disable Stepper Motors
codes.description: Disables (de-energizes) all stepper motors. Axes can be moved freely by hand. Machine position may be lost.
codes.notes: WARNING: Disabling motors causes the machine to lose its homed position. Re-homing (G28) will be required before running a job.

codes.name: Disable Stepper Motors (Alias)
codes.description: Alias for M18. Disables all stepper motors.
codes.notes: Functionally identical to M18. Common in 3D printing G-code end scripts.

codes.name: List SD Card Files
codes.description: Lists files on the SD card.
codes.notes: Smoothieware stores config and G-code files on the onboard SD card. Configuration is via config file on SD rather than $ commands like grbl.

codes.name: Initialize SD Card
codes.description: Initializes the SD card. Usually performed automatically at boot.

codes.name: Release SD Card
codes.description: Releases the SD card so it can be safely removed or accessed by the host.

codes.name: Select SD File
codes.description: Selects a file from the SD card for printing.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Path to file on SD card

codes.name: Start/Resume SD Print
codes.description: Starts or resumes printing the selected SD card file.

examples.description: Start or resume SD card print

codes.name: Pause SD Print
codes.description: Pauses the current SD card print.

codes.name: Set SD Position
codes.description: Sets the file position for the next read from the SD card. Used for resuming prints at a specific position.

examples.description: Reset SD file position to beginning

codes.name: Report SD Print Status
codes.description: Reports the current SD card print progress (bytes read / total bytes).

codes.name: Delete SD File
codes.description: Deletes a file from the SD card.

codes.name: Select and Start SD File
codes.description: Selects a file from the SD card and immediately starts printing it. Combines M23 and M24.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Path to file on SD card

codes.notes: Convenience command combining file selection and print start.

codes.name: Extruder Absolute Mode
codes.description: Sets the extruder to absolute positioning mode. E values are interpreted as absolute positions. Independent from G90/G91.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior — extruder mode is independent from XYZ distance mode.

codes.notes: IMPORTANT: Extruder distance mode (M82/M83) is independent from XYZ distance mode (G90/G91). This is a key difference from grbl where G90/G91 affects all axes. Most slicers output M82 at the start of G-code.

codes.name: Extruder Relative Mode
codes.description: Sets the extruder to relative positioning mode. E values are interpreted as distances to extrude. Independent from G90/G91.
codes.notes: When in relative extruder mode, each E value specifies how much filament to extrude for that move, rather than an absolute position. Eliminates the need for G92 E0 resets.

codes.name: Set Steps Per Unit
codes.description: Sets the steps per millimeter for each axis. Overrides the config file values temporarily (use M500 to save).

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: Steps per mm for X axis

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Steps per mm for Y axis

parameters.name: Z
parameters.description: Steps per mm for Z axis

crossReferences.note: grbl uses $ settings ($100=X steps/mm, $101=Y, $102=Z). Smoothieware uses M92 command or config file (alpha_steps_per_mm, etc.).

codes.notes: Changes are temporary unless saved with M500. The permanent way to set this is in the config file on SD card (alpha_steps_per_mm, beta_steps_per_mm, gamma_steps_per_mm, extruder.hotend.steps_per_mm). This is a key difference from grbl which uses $ commands stored in EEPROM.

codes.name: Set Extruder Temperature
codes.description: Sets the target temperature for the extruder hotend. Returns immediately without waiting for the temperature to be reached.
codes.notes: Non-blocking — returns immediately. Use M109 to wait for the temperature to be reached. Requires the TemperatureControl module.

codes.name: Report Temperatures
codes.description: Reports the current and target temperatures for all configured temperature controllers (hotend, bed, etc.).
codes.notes: Response format: ok T:XXX /YYY @ZZZ (where T=current temp, /=target, @=PWM output). Hosts typically poll this periodically for temperature monitoring.

codes.name: Set Extruder Temperature and Wait
codes.description: Sets the target temperature for the extruder hotend and waits until the temperature is reached before continuing.

examples.description: Turn off hotend and wait for cooldown

codes.notes: Blocking command — G-code execution pauses until the target temperature is reached. Commonly used in start G-code before printing begins.

codes.name: Fan On
codes.description: Turns on the part cooling fan at the specified speed.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Fan speed (0-255, where 255 is full speed)

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. RepRapFirmware also supports additional parameters for thermostatic mode.

codes.notes: Requires the Switch module configured for fan control. S range is 0-255.

codes.name: Fan Off
codes.description: Turns off the part cooling fan. Equivalent to M106 S0.

codes.name: Emergency Stop
codes.description: Immediately shuts down the machine. All motors are disabled, all heaters are turned off, and the system halts. Requires M999 to recover.

crossReferences.note: grbl uses Ctrl-X (0x18) for soft reset. M112 is the standard G-code emergency stop recognized by both firmwares.

codes.notes: CRITICAL: This halts the machine immediately. All heaters off, all motors off. The machine enters a HALT state and will not respond to commands until M999 is issued or the board is power-cycled. Use for genuine emergencies only.

codes.name: Report Current Position
codes.description: Reports the current position of all axes in both work and machine coordinates.

crossReferences.note: grbl uses the ? realtime command for position reporting. M114 is the G-code standard way.

codes.notes: Reports work coordinates (with WCS offsets applied) and can include machine coordinates. Useful for debugging position issues.

codes.name: Report Endstop Status
codes.description: Reports the current state (triggered/not triggered) of all configured endstops.
codes.notes: Useful for diagnosing homing and endstop wiring issues. Reports each endstop as 'triggered' or 'not triggered'.

codes.name: Set Bed Temperature
codes.description: Sets the target temperature for the heated bed. Returns immediately without waiting.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Target bed temperature in degrees Celsius

codes.notes: Non-blocking — returns immediately. Use M190 to wait for temperature.

codes.name: Set Bed Temperature and Wait
codes.description: Sets the target temperature for the heated bed and waits until the temperature is reached.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Target bed temperature in degrees Celsius

codes.notes: Blocking command — G-code execution pauses until bed temperature is reached. Bed heating is slower than hotend, so this can take several minutes.

codes.name: Set Filament Diameter / Volumetric Extrusion
codes.description: Sets the filament diameter for volumetric extrusion mode. When enabled, E values are interpreted as cubic millimeters of filament rather than linear distance.

parameters.name: D
parameters.description: Filament diameter in mm (0 to disable volumetric mode)

codes.notes: When volumetric mode is active, the firmware automatically converts volume (mm³) to linear E distance based on the filament cross-section area.

codes.name: Set Firmware Retraction Parameters
codes.description: Configures the parameters used by firmware retraction (G10 command). Sets retraction length, speed, and Z-lift.
codes.notes: These parameters are used when G10 (retract) is executed. Allows tuning retraction without re-slicing.

codes.name: Set Firmware Unretraction Parameters
codes.description: Configures parameters for firmware un-retraction (G11 command). Can set extra prime length and speed.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Extra unretraction length (added to M207 retraction length) in mm

examples.description: Set 0.05mm extra prime at 2400mm/min unretraction speed

codes.notes: The extra prime length (S) is added to the retraction length from M207 to compensate for oozing during retraction.

codes.name: Set Speed Override / Feed Rate Percentage
codes.description: Sets a speed factor override as a percentage of the programmed feed rate.

crossReferences.note: grbl uses realtime feed override characters (0x91-0x9D). Smoothieware uses the M220 G-code command.

codes.notes: Affects all feed rate moves (G1, G2, G3). Does not affect rapid moves (G0). Similar to the feed rate override knob on a CNC machine.

codes.name: Set Flow Rate Override / Extrusion Percentage
codes.description: Sets an extrusion flow rate override as a percentage. In laser mode, this also controls laser power scaling.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Flow rate percentage (100 = normal, in laser mode: laser power scale)

examples.description: In laser mode: scale laser power to 50%

crossReferences.note: grbl uses realtime spindle override characters (0x99-0x9C). Smoothieware uses M221 for laser power scaling.
Same behavior for extrusion. RepRapFirmware does not overload M221 for laser power.

modeNotes.description: Scales the extrusion amount (E values) by the given percentage.
Scales the laser power output by the given percentage. M221 S50 would halve all laser power values.

codes.notes: Dual-purpose command: controls extrusion flow in 3D printing mode, and laser power scaling in laser mode. This overloading can be surprising when switching between modes.

codes.name: Set PID Parameters
codes.description: Sets the PID controller parameters for a temperature controller (hotend or bed).
codes.notes: PID values can also be set in the config file. Use M303 to auto-tune PID parameters, then M301 or M500 to save them.

codes.name: PID Auto-Tune
codes.description: Runs a PID auto-tuning cycle for a temperature controller. Oscillates the heater and measures response to calculate optimal PID values.
codes.notes: Takes several minutes to complete. After tuning, use M500 to save the new PID values to the config override file. Tune at the temperature you typically print at for best results.

codes.name: Save Settings
codes.description: Saves current settings to the config-override file on the SD card. Saved settings are loaded on boot if 'config-override' is enabled.

crossReferences.note: grbl stores settings in EEPROM via $ commands. Smoothieware saves to a config-override file on SD card. Fundamentally different storage approach.
Same behavior — saves to config-override.g file.

codes.notes: KEY ARCHITECTURAL DIFFERENCE: Smoothieware uses a config file on SD card for settings, not EEPROM $ commands like grbl. M500 saves overrides to /sd/config-override. The config-override file is sourced at boot if enabled in the main config. Edit the main config file directly for permanent changes.

codes.name: Load Settings
codes.description: Loads settings from the config-override file on the SD card.
codes.notes: Loads the /sd/config-override file. This is automatically done at boot if enabled.

codes.name: Report Settings
codes.description: Reports the current configuration settings.

crossReferences.note: grbl uses $$ to display settings. Smoothieware uses M503.

codes.notes: Useful for verifying current settings. Shows values from both the main config and any config-override values.

codes.name: Suspend Print / Filament Change
codes.description: Suspends the current print. Saves the current position, retracts filament, and moves to a park position. Used for filament changes or manual intervention.
codes.notes: The suspend behavior (retraction amount, park position, custom G-code) is configurable via the after_suspend_gcode config setting. Use M601 to resume after the filament change.

codes.name: Resume Print
codes.description: Resumes a suspended print. Restores the saved position and continues execution.
codes.notes: Executes the before_resume_gcode config settings (typically primes the nozzle), then restores position and continues printing.

codes.name: Delta Configuration
codes.description: Sets delta geometry parameters: arm length, delta radius, and other delta-specific values.

parameters.name: L
parameters.description: Arm length (diagonal rod length) in mm

examples.description: Set arm length 250mm, radius 120mm, max height 300mm

codes.notes: Delta kinematics only. These parameters are critical for print accuracy. Use G32 for auto-calibration, or set manually. Save with M500.

codes.name: Delta Endstop Offsets
codes.description: Sets the endstop trim/offset values for each delta tower. Used to fine-tune delta calibration.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: X tower (A tower) endstop offset in mm

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Y tower (B tower) endstop offset in mm

parameters.name: Z
parameters.description: Z tower (C tower) endstop offset in mm

examples.description: Set endstop trim offsets for each tower

codes.notes: Delta kinematics only. Small adjustments to endstop positions to level the print surface. G32 auto-calibration sets these automatically.

codes.name: Set Motor Current
codes.description: Sets the stepper motor current in milliamps. Smoothieware boards have built-in stepper drivers with digitally adjustable current.

examples.description: Set motor currents for all axes

crossReferences.note: grbl does not control motor current — it is set with physical potentiometers or jumpers on external stepper drivers. Smoothieware's integrated drivers allow software-controlled current.

codes.notes: A key advantage of Smoothieware boards — motor current is digitally controlled rather than requiring physical potentiometer adjustment. Current values are also set in the config file (alpha_current, beta_current, gamma_current, delta_current). Typical values: 600-1200mA depending on motor size.

codes.name: Clear Emergency Stop / Reset
codes.description: Clears the HALT state caused by M112 (emergency stop) and resets the firmware to a ready state.

crossReferences.note: grbl uses Ctrl-X for soft reset. Smoothieware uses M999 to clear the HALT state.

codes.notes: After M999, the machine is in a reset state — axes are not homed. You must re-home (G28) before running any job. This is the only way to recover from M112 without power-cycling the board.
